insight: Mono-red units are restricted by a design rule to absorbing no more than 1 damage. This rule explains why Animus has only 2 HP, and is considered violated by Amporilla since Ampo vs. 2 Gauss Cannons is a draw — making it the only non-blue unit capable of that matchup (excluding Xaetron/Mahar regeneration).
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of design rules for mono-red units during a unit creation contest thread.

insight: Stamina is primarily a tool to balance units more carefully or to allow unit spawning, not an intrinsically exciting mechanic in itself. The design constraint for stamina units is that a stamina-depleted unit must still be useful for something, and blocking is the easiest way to satisfy this — which is why all stamina units in the game at the time were blockers.
author: consensus
context: Design discussion about stamina mechanics and green stamina units during unit creation contest.

insight: Larger, more expensive drone variants (like Vivid Drone iterations) are problematic in design because they make openings more rigid, reducing strategic flexibility in the early game.
author: wilson0862
context: Critique of a fan-designed 'Wilder Drone' unit, referencing lessons learned from Vivid Drone design iterations.

insight: A key design principle in Prismata is that the board cannot have unlimited units; supply limits on all units are considered a nearly inflexible design rule to prevent runaway board states.
author: http404error
context: Discussion of general design principles during and after the SDR-breaking unit creation contest.

insight: The proposed custom 'Antima Drone' (generating 3 Blue and 3 Red each turn) was identified as potentially broken in combination with units like Antima Comet and Colossus due to the sheer volume of colored resources generated, while being near-useless without such synergies.
author: f300xen
context: Community evaluation of a fan-designed unit that generates abundant colored resources with a waste-penalty drawback.

insight: In Prismata, standard unit supply values are 1, 4, 10, or 20 — deviating from these is considered non-standard for unit design.
author: f300xen
context: Feedback on a fan-designed unit with an unusual supply value of 2 during a unit creation discussion.

insight: Galvani Drone is a notable exception among frontline units in that it is not a blocker, unlike all other frontline units which are blockers by default.
author: ussgordoncaptain
context: Clarification during discussion of a fan-designed unit about which frontline units are blockers.

insight: A fan-designed Leviathan unit (requiring Animus and 4 Red to buy) was assessed as potentially playable with Nitrocybe as an enabler, since Nitrocybe provides a steady way to absorb excess Red cost. Husks were also identified as viable enablers when the opponent is attacking hard enough that Husks are used promptly, as their effective cost premium for eliminating Red works out to only a fraction of a gold per turn.
author: .bky_1556
context: Detailed cost analysis of a fan-designed high-Red-cost unit to assess playability with various enablers.
